saunas.name: Löyly Helsinki
saunas.description: An architectural masterpiece on the Helsinki waterfront. Löyly offers exceptional wood-burning and smoke saunas with direct access to the Baltic Sea.
saunas.openingHours: Monday: 11 AM to 11 PM, Tuesday: 11 AM to 11 PM, Wednesday: 11 AM to 11 PM, Thursday: 11 AM to 11 PM, Friday: 11 AM to 12 AM, Saturday: 11 AM to 12 AM, Sunday: 11 AM to 10 PM

editorial.whySpecial: An architectural masterpiece on the Helsinki waterfront. Löyly offers exceptional wood-burning and smoke saunas with direct access to the Baltic Sea.
editorial.whatToExpect: Löyly provides a unique sauna experience with its wood-burning and smoke saunas, as well as direct access to the Baltic Sea.
editorial.bestTimeToGo: The best time to visit Löyly is during the summer months when the weather is mild and the Baltic Sea is warm enough for swimming.
editorial.whoItsFor: Löyly is ideal for anyone seeking a unique and authentic sauna experience in a stunning architectural setting, as well as those interested in Finnish sauna culture.
drawbacks: The saunas can get quite crowded, especially on weekends and during peak hours.
The water in the Baltic Sea can be quite cold, even in the summer.
tips: Arrive early to secure a spot in the saunas.
Bring a towel and swimsuit to fully enjoy the experience.

saunas.admission: Admission prices range from €15 to €25 per person, depending on the day and time of visit.

saunas.name: The Well
saunas.description: The largest spa in the Nordics. A massive playground for heat enthusiasts with distinct themed saunas and world-class Aufguss rituals.
saunas.openingHours: Mon-Fri 10-22, Sat-Sun 8-22

editorial.whySpecial: The largest spa in the Nordics, with a massive playground of 15 distinct themed saunas and world-class Aufguss rituals.
editorial.whatToExpect: A luxurious spa experience with 10 unique pools, a cold plunge pool, and a range of invigorating sauna and Aufguss rituals.
editorial.whoItsFor: Heat enthusiasts and those seeking a high-end wellness experience.
editorial.bestTimeToGo: The Well is open year-round, but the best time to visit may be during the colder winter months when the contrast between the hot saunas and the chilly outdoor environment is most invigorating.
drawbacks: The Well can get quite crowded, especially on weekends and holidays.
Prices may be on the higher end for some visitors.
Navigating the large facility and finding your way around can be a bit overwhelming at first.
tips: Arrive early to avoid crowds and secure your preferred sauna spots.
Bring a water bottle and stay hydrated throughout your visit.
Take advantage of the Aufguss rituals to get the most out of the sauna experience.

saunas.name: Ribersborgs Kallbadhus
saunas.description: Iconic open-air bathhouse from 1898. Separate men's and women's sections with nude bathing and direct ocean access.

saunas.admission: €90 per visit, €750 for 10 visits, €1700 for 6 months, €3200 for 1 year

etiquette.towelPolicy: Sit on a towel, never directly on the bench

editorial.whySpecial: Iconic open-air bathhouse from 1898 with separate men's and women's sections, nude bathing, and direct ocean access.
editorial.whatToExpect: Enjoy the warmth of the wood-burning saunas and the refreshing ocean swimming, with stunning views of the Malmö sunset.
editorial.bestTimeToGo: Summer
editorial.whoItsFor: Couples, families, and anyone looking for a unique and historic bathing experience
drawbacks: Can get crowded during peak hours
Separate sections for men and women
tips: Bring your own towel and swimsuit
Arrive early to secure a spot
Bring cash for the entrance fee

saunas.name: Therme Erding

editorial.whoItsFor: Sauna enthusiasts and families looking for a unique thermal spa experience.
editorial.bestTimeToGo: Summer and winter are both great times to visit, as the saunas and thermal pools are enjoyable year-round.

Some areas may feel overwhelming for those seeking a more relaxed experience
Entrance fees can be on the pricier side

drawbacks: Mediterana can be quite expensive, especially for the high-end treatments and packages.
The facility can get quite crowded, especially on weekends and holidays.
The Moorish architecture and design may not appeal to everyone's tastes.
tips: Book your visit in advance, especially during peak seasons.
Consider visiting on a weekday to avoid the crowds.
Take advantage of the various wellness activities and treatments offered, such as the sauna ceremonies and thermal water experiences.

tips: Arrive early to secure a spot in the panorama sauna.

saunas.name: Awana Spa
saunas.description: Bringing European-style sauna theater and Aufguss to the Las Vegas strip.

editorial.whySpecial: Awana Spa is the first of its kind, complete with personalized treatments, immersive facilities and approachable wellness offerings. It draws upon the principles of the golden ratio with rounded rooms, curved walls and highly thought out designs to promote a transformative and holistic experience.
editorial.whatToExpect: Guests can choose from a variety of treatments inspired by European and Eastern rituals with a unique social approach. A unique offering includes a Sauna-Meister-led journey that enhances your visit as they direct hot and scented steam towards you through the rhythmical movements of a towel.
highlights: Personalized treatments inspired by European and Eastern rituals
Immersive facilities and holistic wellness offerings
editorial.whoItsFor: Couples, groups, and individuals seeking a unique and immersive wellness experience
drawbacks: Can be crowded during peak hours
Treatments can be expensive
tips: Arrive early to secure a spot in the sauna theater
Bring a swimsuit or robe for the treatments

saunas.admission: Treatments range from $100 to $500 per person

editorial.whoItsFor: Haeckels House is suitable for anyone seeking a relaxing and rejuvenating wellness experience, whether alone or with friends.
editorial.whoShouldSkip: There are no specific groups that should skip Haeckels House, as it caters to a wide range of visitors.
highlights: Saunas and cold plunge pools overlooking the sea
Relaxation areas for unwinding and recharging
Commitment to eco-friendly and ethical practices
drawbacks: Limited availability of appointments and treatments
Relatively high prices for the experience

saunas.openingHours: Every day 07.00-22.00 (when weather and sunshine hours allow)

editorial.whySpecial: Located in a nature reserve just 15 mins from Stockholm city. The go-to place for a lake dip after sauna.
editorial.whatToExpect: Hellasgården offers a variety of activities and facilities, including swimming, saunas, tennis, cycling, hiking, and more. It provides a balance between the comforts of the city and the tranquility of nature.
editorial.bestTimeToGo: Summer
editorial.whoItsFor: Families, nature lovers, outdoor enthusiasts
drawbacks: Can get crowded on hot summer days
tips: Bring swimwear and towel
Arrive early to secure a spot

saunas.admission: Free entry, fees for some activities

saunas.name: Yasuragi
saunas.description: A massive Japanese ryokan-style spa in the Swedish archipelago. Everyone wears matching yukatas.
saunas.openingHours: Mon-Fri 10-22, Sat-Sun 8-22

editorial.whySpecial: A massive Japanese ryokan-style spa in the Swedish archipelago, where everyone wears matching yukatas.
editorial.whatToExpect: Relaxation in the Japanese bath, participation in beneficial activities, and exquisite meals inspired by Japanese cuisine in one of the three restaurants.
tips: Take the boat from Strömkajen to Hasseludden's pier and enjoy the summer evening on the rooftop terrace.
editorial.bestTimeToGo: Spring and summer are the best times to visit, when the weather is mild and the surrounding nature is in full bloom.
editorial.whoItsFor: Yasuragi is ideal for those seeking a relaxing and rejuvenating spa experience with a touch of Japanese culture. It's perfect for couples, families, and groups looking to unwind and immerse themselves in a unique atmosphere.
editorial.whoShouldSkip: Those who are not interested in Japanese culture or prefer a more traditional European-style spa experience may not find Yasuragi as appealing.
drawbacks: The location may be inconvenient for some, as it is situated in the Swedish archipelago, requiring a bit of travel to reach.
The prices at Yasuragi can be on the higher end, making it less accessible for budget-conscious travelers.
The Japanese-inspired design and atmosphere may not appeal to everyone, especially those who prefer a more modern or Western-style spa setting.

saunas.name: CopenHot
saunas.description: Wood-fired hot tubs and saunas right on the Copenhagen harbor front. Very social and lively.
saunas.admission: €130 per hot tub, €130 per sauna, €35 per person for HotDays

etiquette.sessionLength: 1.5 hours for hot tubs and saunas, 1.5 hours for HotDays

editorial.whySpecial: Wood-fired hot tubs and saunas right on the Copenhagen harbor front with great views. Very social and lively atmosphere.
editorial.whatToExpect: Relax in hot tubs and saunas, enjoy cold plunge tubs, and socialize with other guests. Drinks can be purchased from the bar.
editorial.whoItsFor: Anyone looking for a unique and social sauna/hot tub experience in Copenhagen
tips: Bring your own beer or wine (no outside food allowed)
Arrive early to secure a spot during busy times

saunas.name: La Source Bains Nordiques

location.address: 4200 Rue Forest Hill, Rawdon, QC J0K 1S0, Canada

saunas.description: Carved into the side of a mountain, this spa uses modern architecture to frame the raw Canadian wilderness.

editorial.whySpecial: Carved into the side of a mountain, this spa uses modern architecture to frame the raw Canadian wilderness.
editorial.whatToExpect: Visitors can enjoy therapeutic massages, Nordic baths, relaxation areas, and a bistro featuring local products, all in the lush forest of Rawdon.

saunas.name: H2O Adventure Spa

location.address: 4075 Gordon Dr, Kelowna, BC V1W 5J2, Canada

saunas.description: A massive aquatic centre that includes significant wellness facilities, making it accessible for families.
saunas.openingHours: Monday: 5:30 AM to 9 PM, Tuesday: 5:30 AM to 9 PM, Wednesday: 5:30 AM to 9 PM, Thursday: 5:30 AM to 9 PM, Friday: 5:30 AM to 9 PM, Saturday: 8 AM to 8 PM, Sunday: 8 AM to 8 PM

editorial.whySpecial: A massive aquatic centre that includes significant wellness facilities, making it accessible for families.
editorial.whatToExpect: The facility includes waterslides, a wave pool, lazy river, splash park, cardio and conditioning areas, and functional training equipment. Members can access YMCA programming like health coaching and fitness classes.
editorial.whoItsFor: Families, fitness enthusiasts
tips: Respect facility rules and etiquette
Check schedules before visiting
Children under 8 must be within arm's reach of an adult

saunas.name: Thermae Bath Spa

location.address: The Hetling Pump Room, Hot Bath St, Bath BA1 1SJ, United Kingdom

saunas.description: Britain's only natural thermal spa. The rooftop pool offers incredible views over the historic city of Bath.
saunas.openingHours: Monday: 9 AM to 9 PM, Tuesday: 9 AM to 9 PM, Wednesday: 9 AM to 9 PM, Thursday: 9 AM to 9 PM, Friday: 9 AM to 9 PM, Saturday: 9 AM to 9 PM, Sunday: 9 AM to 9 PM

editorial.whySpecial: Britain's only natural thermal spa, with a rooftop pool offering incredible views over the historic city of Bath.
editorial.whatToExpect: Relax in the mineral-rich thermal waters, enjoy treatments, and take in the stunning views from the rooftop pool.
editorial.whoItsFor: Anyone looking for a relaxing spa experience in a historic setting.
tips: Arrive early to make the most of the facilities
Consider booking a treatment package for the full experience

saunas.name: Rudding Park Spa

location.address: 1JH, Rudding Ln, Harrogate HG3 1LL, United Kingdom

saunas.description: Award-winning luxury spa with a focus on bringing the outdoors in. Known for its rooftop spa garden.
saunas.openingHours: Monday: 6:30 AM to 10:30 PM, Tuesday: 6:30 AM to 10:30 PM, Wednesday: 6:30 AM to 10:30 PM, Thursday: 6:30 AM to 10:30 PM, Friday: 6:30 AM to 10:30 PM, Saturday: 6:30 AM to 10:30 PM, Sunday: 6:30 AM to 10:30 PM
saunas.admission: From £66 per person for Roof Top Spa experiences, from £148 per person for spa days with treatments

editorial.whySpecial: Award-winning luxury spa with a focus on bringing the outdoors in. Known for its rooftop spa garden.
editorial.whatToExpect: The Roof Top Spa is a social space with a range of hydrotherapy & thermal experiences and the Escape Zone, a peaceful, serene space for guests having a treatment. Guests can also maintain their beauty routine in the Nail & Beauty Studio, eat, drink & shop.

saunas.name: Spa Zuiver
saunas.description: A massive wellness complex on the edge of the Amsterdam forest. High standards and textile-free days.
saunas.openingHours: Mon-Sun 24 hours

editorial.whySpecial: A massive wellness complex on the edge of the Amsterdam forest with high standards and textile-free days.
editorial.whatToExpect: Relax in the sauna, heated pools, and serene relaxation areas. Enjoy daily rituals, soundjourneys, and professional treatments.
editorial.whoItsFor: Anyone looking for a wellness retreat with a balance of relaxation and activity.
highlights: Large complex with sauna, pools, and relaxation areas

saunas.name: Sauna Shikiji

location.address: 2 Chome-25-1 Shikiji, Suruga Ward, Shizuoka, 422-8036, Japan

saunas.description: Known as the 'Holy Land' for saunners in Japan. Famous for its natural groundwater cold plunge which is drinkable.
saunas.openingHours: Monday: Open 24 hours, Tuesday: Open 24 hours, Wednesday: Open 24 hours, Thursday: Open 24 hours, Friday: Open 24 hours, Saturday: Open 24 hours, Sunday: Open 24 hours

etiquette.towelPolicy: Towels and robes should not be taken home.

editorial.whySpecial: Known as the 'Holy Land' for saunners in Japan. Famous for its natural groundwater cold plunge which is drinkable.
editorial.whatToExpect: The sauna features a sacred natural spring water cold plunge that is drinkable. It is said to have various health benefits such as improving skin conditions, circulation, and digestion.

saunas.name: Skyspa Yokohama
saunas.description: Located on the 14th floor with stunning views of Yokohama Bay. A hub for modern Japanese sauna culture.
saunas.openingHours: Mon-Fri 10-22, Sat-Sun 8-22

editorial.whySpecial: Located on the 14th floor with stunning views of Yokohama Bay. A hub for modern Japanese sauna culture.
editorial.whatToExpect: The spa offers a range of facilities including saunas, jacuzzis, and massage services. Guests can also use the coworking spaces.
editorial.bestTimeToGo: The spa is open year-round, but the best time to visit may be during the warmer months when you can fully enjoy the stunning views of Yokohama Bay.
editorial.whoItsFor: Skyspa Yokohama is ideal for those interested in modern Japanese sauna culture, as well as remote workers who can take advantage of the coworking spaces.
drawbacks: The spa may be crowded during peak hours, especially on weekends.
Prices for some services may be on the higher end.
tips: Arrive early to secure a spot in the saunas and enjoy the views during less crowded times.
Consider visiting on a weekday if possible to avoid the weekend crowds.

saunas.name: Blue Lagoon
saunas.description: World-famous geothermal spa located in a lava field. The milky blue water is rich in minerals like silica and sulfur.
saunas.openingHours: Monday: 8 AM to 8 PM, Tuesday: 8 AM to 8 PM, Wednesday: 8 AM to 8 PM, Thursday: 8 AM to 8 PM, Friday: 8 AM to 8 PM, Saturday: 8 AM to 8 PM, Sunday: 8 AM to 8 PM

editorial.whySpecial: World-famous geothermal spa located in a lava field. The milky blue water is rich in minerals like silica and sulfur.
editorial.whatToExpect: Visitors can enjoy the healing geothermal waters, relax in the spa, and experience the unique natural environment.

saunas.name: Secret Lagoon
saunas.description: Iceland's oldest swimming pool, made in 1891. A more authentic and less crowded alternative to the Blue Lagoon.
saunas.openingHours: Winter season: 1st of October to 31st of May, open daily 10:00 to 19:00 (closed 24th and 31st of December at 18:00); Summer season: 1st of June to 30th of September, open daily 10:00 to 20:00
saunas.admission: Adults (15+): €35, Children (14-): €2, Seniors (67+) and people with disabilities: €26

editorial.whySpecial: The Secret Lagoon is the oldest swimming pool in Iceland, made in 1891, and features natural geothermal hot springs that provide the water supply.
editorial.whatToExpect: A more authentic and less crowded alternative to the Blue Lagoon, with a relaxed atmosphere and the opportunity to see an active geyser.
editorial.bestTimeToGo: The summer season from June to September is the best time to visit the Secret Lagoon, as the weather is milder and the pool is open for longer hours.
editorial.whoItsFor: The Secret Lagoon is ideal for those seeking a more authentic and less crowded geothermal experience in Iceland, as well as for families and those interested in the country's history and natural wonders.
drawbacks: The pool can get crowded during peak tourist seasons
The water temperature may be too hot for some visitors
tips: Bring a swimsuit, towel, and water shoes
Arrive early to avoid crowds
Check the geyser eruption schedule to time your visit

saunas.name: Iglu Park
saunas.description: A cool village of Iglucraft saunas right on the Baltic Sea in the Noblessner district.

etiquette.towelPolicy: Wear swimwear or a towel in the shared sauna

editorial.whySpecial: Iglupark is a unique concept that combines work, leisure, and health in a well-thought-out whole, offering the opportunity to take time for yourself by visiting the iguoffice, iglucabin, and iglusauna with stunning views of the Baltic Sea.
editorial.whatToExpect: Visitors can enjoy the endless horizon, fresh air, comfortable terrace, and time for themselves, alone or with friends and loved ones. The iglusaunas offer a view of Tallinn Bay and have a spacious front room, private terrace, and access to a swimming area.
editorial.whoItsFor: Iglupark is suitable for individuals, couples, and groups looking for a unique sauna experience with stunning sea views in Tallinn.

saunas.name: Kalma Saun
saunas.description: One of Tallinn's oldest public saunas. A truly authentic experience with massive wood-burning ovens.
saunas.openingHours: Monday: 11 AM to 10 PM, Tuesday: 11 AM to 10 PM, Wednesday: 11 AM to 10 PM, Thursday: 11 AM to 10 PM, Friday: 11 AM to 10 PM, Saturday: 10 AM to 11 PM, Sunday: 10 AM to 11 PM

editorial.whySpecial: One of Tallinn's oldest public saunas with a truly authentic experience and massive wood-burning ovens.
editorial.whatToExpect: A stately sauna with a dignified atmosphere and history dating back to 1928, despite several renovations over the years.

saunas.name: Bondi Icebergs

location.address: 1 Notts Ave, Bondi Beach NSW 2026, Australia

saunas.description: The most famous pool in Australia. The sauna overlooks the pool where waves crash over the side.
saunas.openingHours: Monday: 6 AM to 6:30 PM, Tuesday: 6 AM to 6:30 PM, Wednesday: 6 AM to 6:30 PM, Thursday: Closed, Friday: 6 AM to 6:30 PM, Saturday: 6 AM to 6:30 PM, Sunday: 6 AM to 6:30 PM

editorial.whySpecial: The Bondi Icebergs is the most famous pool in Australia, with a sauna that overlooks the pool where waves crash over the side.
editorial.whatToExpect: Visitors can enjoy a casual bistro, two licensed bars, and dedicated function facilities at the Bondi Icebergs Club, which overlooks the sparkling blue waters of the Bondi Icebergs pool and North Bondi headland.
editorial.bestTimeToGo: The best time to visit the Bondi Icebergs is during the summer months when the weather is warm and the ocean is at its most inviting.
editorial.whoItsFor: The Bondi Icebergs is ideal for anyone who enjoys swimming, relaxing, and taking in stunning ocean views. It's a popular destination for both locals and tourists.
drawbacks: The pool can get quite crowded during peak times, especially on weekends and holidays.
The water can be quite cold, even during the summer months.
The sauna may be small and crowded at times.
tips: Arrive early in the morning or late in the afternoon to avoid the crowds.
Bring a towel and swimsuit, as well as some warm clothing for the sauna.
Consider visiting on a weekday if possible to avoid the busiest times.

saunas.admission: AUD $8 for adults, AUD $4 for children

saunas.name: Peninsula Hot Springs
saunas.description: Award-winning natural geothermal mineral springs and day spa. The hilltop pool offers 360-degree views.
saunas.openingHours: Mon-Fri 10-22, Sat-Sun 8-22

etiquette.towelPolicy: Towel, robe and locker hire included
etiquette.sessionLength: Varies by package, up to 165 minutes

editorial.whySpecial: Award-winning natural geothermal mineral springs and day spa with over 70 bathing and wellness experiences, including a hilltop pool with 360-degree views.
editorial.whatToExpect: Immerse yourself in nature as you journey through the geothermal bathing and wellness experiences. Relax in the Bath House or retreat to the adults-only Spa Dreaming Centre. Enjoy signature spa treatments and dining experiences.
editorial.whoItsFor: Families, friends, couples, and first-time visitors looking for a rejuvenating spa experience in nature.
editorial.bestTimeToGo: The best time to visit Peninsula Hot Springs is during the spring and autumn months when the weather is mild and comfortable.
drawbacks: It can get quite crowded, especially on weekends and holidays.
The cost of admission and treatments can be on the higher side.
The facilities can feel a bit commercialized for some visitors seeking a more natural experience.
tips: Arrive early to avoid crowds and secure a spot in the popular hilltop pool.
Bring a towel, swimsuit, and sandals to make the most of your visit.
Consider booking treatments or packages in advance to ensure availability.
